summary: Alfred is a well-architected Rust text editor with strong documentation (6 ADRs), compile-time enforced crate boundaries, and a proven plugin-first architecture. The pure functional core enables 94 core tests with zero mocking. The top hotspot (app.rs) is driven by test mass rather than production complexity.

details.readme_note: No top-level README.md; project conventions are in CLAUDE.md
details.inline_docs: All public functions and modules have doc comments. Most include usage semantics and edge case documentation.

details.dependency_direction: All dependencies point inward toward alfred-core
details.boundary_enforcement: Cargo workspace enforces crate boundaries at compile time
details.naming_convention: snake_case for functions, CamelCase for types, kebab-case for commands

alfred-core.purpose: Pure domain layer: buffer, cursor, viewport, commands, hooks, keymaps, editor state

alfred-lisp.purpose: Lisp runtime wrapper, bridge primitives, define-command/hook/keymap integration

alfred-plugin.purpose: Plugin discovery, metadata parsing, registry, topological sort, lifecycle

alfred-tui.purpose: Event loop, key conversion, terminal rendering, colon commands

alfred-bin.purpose: Binary entry point, CLI parsing, composition root

test_strategies: Acceptance tests first, unit tests for edge cases
test_analysis.mock_usage: Zero mocks in core tests (pure functions)

top_hotspots.note: ~2700 lines are tests; production code is ~600 lines. Integration point for event loop, key dispatch, colon commands, deferred actions.
Aggregation root with 32 built-in command registrations. Growing as new commands are added.
14 bridge primitives. Each new primitive adds ~50-80 lines. Stable pattern but growing.
15 movement functions. Word-movement algorithms (w/b/e) have moderate complexity.
Stable foundation. Line manipulation functions added for M9 editing commands.

risks.title: app.rs concentration
risks.description: app.rs at 3,301 lines (24 changes) is the convergence point for all new features. However, ~2,700 lines are tests, making production code ~600 lines.
risks.mitigation: Extract colon command dispatch and deferred action execution into submodules

risks.title: editor_state.rs command registration growth
risks.description: 32 built-in commands registered in a single function. Each new command adds ~10 lines of boilerplate.
risks.mitigation: Consider a declarative command registration macro or table-driven registration

risks.title: rust_lisp maintenance risk
risks.description: rust_lisp has ~300 GitHub stars and fewer contributors than Janet. ADR-004 acknowledges this.
risks.mitigation: Documented migration path in ADR-004. rust_lisp is simple enough to fork.

risks.title: No README.md
risks.description: Project conventions are in CLAUDE.md but there is no standard README for new contributors.
risks.mitigation: Create a README.md with build instructions, architecture overview, and links to ADRs

risks.title: PluginRegistry not persisted on EditorState
risks.description: PluginRegistry is created during startup and consumed (not stored). Runtime plugin management requires storing it on EditorState.
risks.mitigation: Comment in main.rs acknowledges this is deferred to when runtime plugin lifecycle is needed

risks.title: Single-buffer limitation
risks.description: EditorState holds a single Buffer. Multi-buffer/multi-tab editing requires architectural evolution.
risks.mitigation: Not needed for current scope. Extension path is clear.

risks.title: No async I/O
risks.description: Single-threaded synchronous execution (ADR-003). Long Lisp expressions would freeze UI.
risks.mitigation: By design for walking skeleton. No operations currently require async.

risks.title: RefCell borrowing fragility
risks.description: Rc<RefCell<EditorState>> shared between event loop and Lisp bridge closures. Incorrect borrow ordering causes panics.
risks.mitigation: DeferredAction pattern and ClonedHandler pattern resolve known borrow conflicts. Two commits explicitly fix RefCell issues.

recommendations.title: Create a README.md
recommendations.description: Add a standard README with build instructions, architecture diagram, and links to the 6 ADRs. CLAUDE.md is not discoverable by new contributors browsing the repository.

recommendations.title: Extract colon command dispatch from app.rs
recommendations.description: The colon command system (execute_colon_command and its match arms) could live in a dedicated module. This would reduce app.rs hotspot score and improve navigability.

recommendations.title: Declarative command registration
recommendations.description: The 32 built-in commands in register_builtin_commands follow a repetitive pattern. A macro or table-driven approach would reduce boilerplate in editor_state.rs.

recommendations.title: Store PluginRegistry on EditorState
recommendations.description: Enable runtime plugin management (reload, unload via colon commands) by persisting the PluginRegistry on EditorState rather than consuming it at startup.

recommendations.title: Add integration test for plugin load order
recommendations.description: While unit tests for topological sort exist, an end-to-end test loading real plugins with dependencies would strengthen confidence in the discovery-to-execution pipeline.

plugins.name: vim-keybindings
plugins.description: Full vim modal editing with normal/insert modes

plugins.name: basic-keybindings
plugins.description: Arrow keys, colon command mode, backspace

plugins.name: line-numbers
plugins.description: Enables gutter line numbers via render-gutter hook

plugins.name: status-bar
plugins.description: Enables status bar via render-status hook

plugins.name: test-plugin
plugins.description: Demo plugin registering a hello command
